summary: Orchestrates message summarization: applies 150-char bypass heuristic, calls LLM via Client interface, persists summaries to storage, processes a today+yesterday time-windowed queue

requires: Non-nil storage.Store with functional Channels() and MessageSummaries() sub-stores
model string identifying the LLM model to tag stored summaries
guarantees: SummarizeMessage always writes a MessageSummary to storage on success (even for short messages copied verbatim)
SummarizeUnsummarized returns a non-error result and records queue stop details in SummarizeQueueResult.LastError — it does NOT propagate API errors to caller
SummarizeUnsummarizedForChannels with nil channelIDs is equivalent to SummarizeUnsummarized

effects: LLM API network call (via Client) for messages >= 150 chars
state: Struct holds client, model string, store reference, and localModel flag — created once via NewService, no mutable fields after construction

observations.text: Short messages (< 150 chars) are copied verbatim as the summary and stored — the LLM is never called; Skipped flag in result distinguishes these from API-summarized messages
Queue processing is strictly sequential and stops immediately on the first API error, recording it in LastError. Partial progress is preserved in storage and reflected in the result counts
Time window is start-of-yesterday UTC to end-of-today UTC, computed fresh on each call — crossing midnight will naturally extend the window without any configuration
If Channels().GetByID() returns an error or nil channel, the message is silently skipped (continue) without incrementing any counter — missed messages are invisible in the result
Functional options pattern (ServiceOption / WithLocalModel) used for optional configuration — keeps NewService signature stable while allowing provider-specific prompt tuning
Service depends on storage.Store boundary package for both channel metadata lookup and summary persistence — no in-memory fallback; storage unavailability is fatal to the queue
